<content>1410: :dp:`fls_pybv4krsvktv`
1411: :t:`Method resolution` proceeds as follows:
1412: 
1413: #. :dp:`fls_m2njj6no0p1i`
1414:    For each :t:`candidate receiver type` of the
1415:    :t:`candidate receiver type chain`
1416: 
1417:    #. :dp:`fls_16l2q1wpcnbp`
1418:       Perform :t:`method resolution receiver candidate lookup` for the
1419:       :t:`candidate receiver type`.
1420: 
1421:    #. :dp:`fls_fcnahkqxomuo`
1422:       If the last :t:`candidate receiver type` is an :t:`array type`, then
1423:       perform :t:`method resolution receiver candidate lookup` for a
1424:       :t:`slice type` where the :t:`slice type` has the same :t:`element type`
1425:       as the :t:`array type`.
1426: 
1427: :dp:`fls_ii0fdpekn1qt`
1428: :dt:`Method resolution receiver candidate lookup` for a :t:`receiver type`
1429: proceeds as follows:
1430: 
1431: #. :dp:`fls_ohjmxhbw3nx3`
1432:    Perform :t:`method resolution implementation candidate lookup` for the
1433:    :t:`receiver type`.
1434: 
1435: #. :dp:`fls_lgpdicxxwq13`
1436:    Perform :t:`method resolution implementation candidate lookup` for the
1437:    :t:`immutable borrow` of the :t:`receiver type`.
1438: 
1439: #. :dp:`fls_ugl3x4y3lli2`
1440:    Perform :t:`method resolution implementation candidate lookup` for the
1441:    :t:`mutable borrow` of the :t:`receiver type`.
1442: 
1443: :dp:`fls_bb4cbmvui8fk`
1444: :dt:`Method resolution implementation candidate lookup` for a
1445: :t:`receiver type` proceeds as follows:
1446: 
1447: #. :dp:`fls_5wny1yxbyuz0`
1448:    Perform :t:`method resolution inherent implementation candidate lookup` for
1449:    the :t:`receiver type`.
1450: 
1451: #. :dp:`fls_gsc8pt4tlsqv`
1452:    Perform :t:`method resolution trait implementation candidate lookup` for the
1453:    :t:`receiver type`.
1454: 
1455: :dp:`fls_tfglce1wuq5q`
1456: :dt:`Method resolution inherent implementation candidate lookup` for a
1457: :t:`receiver type` proceeds as follows:
1458: 
1459: #. :dp:`fls_64bfcn9okeve`
1460:    Construct the :t:`dereference type chain` for the :t:`receiver type`.
1461: 
1462: #. :dp:`fls_om90v9re8b2l`
1463:    For each :t:`dereference type` in the :t:`dereference type chain`
1464: 
1465:    #. :dp:`fls_bsf4hy9x7c2e`
1466:       For each :dt:`inherent implementation` in the set of
1467:       :t:`[inherent implementation]s` of the :t:`dereference type` where the
1468:       :t:`implementing type` :t:`unifies <unify>` with the
1469:       :t:`dereference type`
1470: 
1471:       #. :dp:`fls_cnn5hkf1z5q4`
1472:          Try to locate a :t:`candidate method` in the :t:`inherent
1473:          implementation`, where the :t:`type` of the :t:`self parameter`
1474:          :t:`unifies <unify>` with the :t:`receiver type`.
1475: 
1476:       #. :dp:`fls_j9ho6xc2fj0w`
1477:          If such a :t:`candidate method` exists, then the
1478:          :t:`method call expression` resolves to that :t:`candidate method` and
1479:          :t:`method resolution` stops.
1480: 
1481: :dp:`fls_1y94elgpg0uk`
1482: :dt:`Method resolution trait implementation candidate lookup` for a
1483: :t:`receiver type` proceeds as follows:
1484: 
1485: #. :dp:`fls_npsdxrtcslcf`
1486:    Construct the :t:`dereference type chain` for the :t:`receiver type`.
1487: 
1488: #. :dp:`fls_yv5l823lwdsv`
1489:    For each :t:`dereference type` in the :t:`dereference type chain`
1490: 
1491:    #. :dp:`fls_ckdoyvbaybe0`
1492:       For each :t:`trait implementation` of the :t:`dereference type` where the
1493:       :t:`implemented trait` is :t:`in scope`
1494: 
1495:       #. :dp:`fls_1azkiu20r0e4`
1496:          Try to locate a :t:`candidate method` in the
1497:          :t:`trait implementation`, where the :t:`type` of the
1498:          :t:`self parameter` :t:`unifies <unify>` with the :t:`receiver type`.
1499: 
1500:       #. :dp:`fls_ose5m4bhkg57`
1501:          If such a :t:`candidate method` exists, then the
1502:          :t:`method call expression` resolves to that :t:`candidate method` and
1503:          :t:`method resolution` stops.
1504: 
1505: :dp:`fls_jw2yv23cduu4`
1506: A :t:`method call expression` shall resolve to exactly one :t:`method`.
1507: 
1508: .. _fls_TelBKNKodx3d:

1510: Call Resolution
1511: ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
1512: 
1513: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
1514: 
1515: :dp:`fls_ZjJ7y9r6QQMW`
1516: :dt:`call resolution` is a form of :t:`resolution` that applies to a
1517: :t:`call expression`.
1518: 
1519: :dp:`fls_zBSloU2Gjv7x`
1520: A :dt:`candidate callee type` is the :t:`type` of the :t:`call operand`
1521: of a :t:`call expression` :t:`under resolution`.
1522: 
1523: :dp:`fls_XBakDylF7aOG`
1524: A :dt:`candidate callee type chain` is a sequence of :t:`[candidate callee type]s`.
1525: The :t:`candidate callee type chain` starts with the :t:`type` of the :t:`call
1526: operand` of the:t:`call expression` :t:`under resolution`. From then on, the
1527: :t:`candidate callee type chain` is treated as a :t:`dereference type chain`.
1528:

8: Functions
9: =========
10: 
11: .. rubric:: Syntax
12: 
13: .. syntax::
14: 
15:    FunctionDeclaration ::=
16:        FunctionQualifierList $$fn$$ Name GenericParameterList? $$($$ FunctionParameterList? $$)$$ ReturnType? WhereClause? (FunctionBody | $$;$$)
17: 
18:    FunctionQualifierList ::=
19:        $$const$$? $$async$$? ItemSafety? AbiSpecification?
20: 
21:    FunctionParameterList ::=
22:        (FunctionParameter ($$,$$ FunctionParameter)* $$,$$?)
23:      | (SelfParameter ($$,$$ FunctionParameter)* $$,$$?)
24: 
25:    FunctionParameter ::=
26:        OuterAttributeOrDoc* (FunctionParameterPattern | FunctionParameterVariadicPart | TypeSpecification)
27: 
28:    FunctionParameterPattern ::=
29:        PatternWithoutAlternation (TypeAscription | ($$:$$ FunctionParameterVariadicPart))
30: 
31:    FunctionParameterVariadicPart ::=
32:        $$...$$
33: 
34:    ReturnType ::=
35:        $$->$$ TypeSpecification
36: 
37:    FunctionBody ::=
38:        BlockExpression
39: 
40:    SelfParameter ::=
41:        OuterAttributeOrDoc* (ShorthandSelf | TypedSelf)
42: 
43:    ShorthandSelf ::=
44:        ($$&$$ LifetimeIndication?)? $$mut$$? $$self$$
45: 
46:    TypedSelf ::=
47:        $$mut$$? $$self$$ TypeAscription
48:

50: 
51: :dp:`fls_gn1ngtx2tp2s`
52: A :t:`function` is a :t:`value` of a :t:`function type` that models a behavior.
53: 
54: :dp:`fls_bdx9gnnjxru3`
55: A :t:`function` declares a unique :t:`function item type` for itself.
56: 
57: :dp:`fls_87jnkimc15gi`
58: A :dt:`function qualifier` is a :t:`construct` that determines the role of
59: a :t:`function`.
60: 
61: :dp:`fls_nwywh1vjt6rr`
62: A :t:`function` shall not be subject to both :t:`keyword` ``async`` and
63: :t:`keyword` ``const``.
64: 
65: :dp:`fls_uwuthzfgslif`
66: A :t:`function parameter` is a :t:`construct` that yields a set of
67: :t:`[binding]s` that bind matched input :t:`[value]s` to :t:`[name]s` at the
68: site of a :t:`call expression` or a :t:`method call expression`.
69: 
70: :dp:`fls_ymeo93t4mz4`
71: A :t:`self parameter` is a :t:`function parameter` expressed by :t:`keyword`
72: ``self``.
73: 
74: :dp:`fls_4jrj1t4jcf8p`
75: A :dt:`method` is an :t:`associated function` with a :t:`self parameter`.
76: 
77: :dp:`fls_ijbt4tgnl95n`
78: A :t:`function` shall not specify a :t:`self parameter` unless it is an
79: :t:`associated function`.
80: 
81: :dp:`fls_AAYJDCNMJgTq`
82: The :t:`type` of a :t:`function parameter` is determined as follows:
83: 
84: * :dp:`fls_PGtp39f6gJwU`
85:   If the :t:`function parameter` is a :t:`self parameter` without a :s:`TypeSpecification`:
86: 
87:   * :dp:`fls_yZ2yIXxmy2ri`
88:     And the :t:`self parameter` has token ``&`` and :t:`keyword` ``mut``, then the :t:`type` is ``&mut Self``.
89: 
90:   * :dp:`fls_35aSvBxBnIzm`
91:     And the :t:`self parameter` has token ``&`` and lacks :t:`keyword` ``mut``, then the :t:`type` is ``&Self``.
92: 
93:   * :dp:`fls_Ogziu8S01qPQ`
94:     And the :t:`self parameter` lacks token ``&`` and :t:`keyword` ``mut``, then the :t:`type` is ``Self``.
95: 
96: * :dp:`fls_xCSsxYUZUFed`
97:   Otherwise the :t:`type` is the specified :t:`type`.
98: 
99: :dp:`fls_lxzinvqveuqh`
100: The :t:`pattern` of a :t:`function parameter` shall be an :t:`irrefutable
101: pattern`.
102: 
103: :dp:`fls_kcAbTPZXQ5Y8`
104: The :t:`expected type` of the :t:`pattern` of a :t:`function parameter` is the :t:`type` of the :t:`function parameter`.
105: 
106: :dp:`fls_PGDKWK7nPvgw`
107: The :t:`[binding]s` of all :t:`[pattern]s` of all :t:`[function parameter]s` of a :t:`function` shall not shadow another.
108: 
109: :dp:`fls_o4uSLPo00KUg`
110: A :dt:`variadic function` is an :t:`external function` that specifies
111: :s:`FunctionParameterVariadicPart` as the last :t:`function parameter`.
112: 
113: :dp:`fls_icdzs1mjh0n4`
114: A :t:`variadic function` shall specify one of the following :t:`[ABI]s`:
115: 
116: * :dp:`fls_OR85NVifPwjr`
117:   ``extern "C"``
118: * :dp:`fls_4s2IdfYDzPrX`
119:   ``extern "C-unwind"``
120: * :dp:`fls_ZJJppPfiJRou`
121:   ``extern "aapcs"``
122: * :dp:`fls_jOyZh9ujWWHQ`
123:   ``extern "aapcs-unwind"``
124: * :dp:`fls_Xdr0bFwxhWiB`
125:   ``extern "cdecl"``
126: * :dp:`fls_DpTFEHZAABdD`
127:   ``extern "cdecl-unwind"``
128: * :dp:`fls_b7FTlWfnX2OI`
129:   ``extern "efiapi"``
130: * :dp:`fls_6urL6fZ5cpaA`
131:   ``extern "system"``
132: * :dp:`fls_TMOzb6cYIOlH`
133:   ``extern "system-unwind"``
134: * :dp:`fls_eHPWHrvs7ETl`
135:   ``extern "sysv64"``
136: * :dp:`fls_mjCrvmikm58M`
137:   ``extern "sysv64-unwind"``
138: * :dp:`fls_4EUb9zFatZ97`
139:   ``extern "win64"``
140: * :dp:`fls_4B4B5FIqAes9`
141:   ``extern "win64-unwind"``
142: 
143: :dp:`fls_vljy4mm0zca2`
144: A :t:`return type` is the :t:`type` of the result a :t:`function`, :t:`closure type` or :t:`function pointer type` returns.
145: 
146: :dp:`fls_EqJb3Jl3vK8K`
147: The :t:`return type` of a :t:`function` is determined as follows:
148: 
149: * :dp:`fls_C7dvzcXcpQCy`
150:   If the :s:`FunctionDeclaration` specifies a :s:`ReturnType`, then the :t:`return type` is the specified :s:`ReturnType`.
151: 
152: * :dp:`fls_J8X8ahnJLrMo`
153:   Otherwise the :t:`return type` is the :t:`unit type`.
154: 
155: :dp:`fls_927nfm5mkbsp`
156: A :dt:`function body` is the :t:`block expression` of a :t:`function`.
157: 
158: :dp:`fls_yfm0jh62oaxr`
159: A :t:`function` shall have a :t:`function body` unless it is an
160: :t:`associated trait function` or an :t:`external function`.
161: 
162: :dp:`fls_bHwy8FLzEUi3`
163: A :t:`function body` denotes a :t:`control flow boundary`.
164: 
165: :dp:`fls_5Q861wb08DU3`
166: A :t:`function body` of an :t:`async function` denotes an
167: :t:`async control flow boundary`.
168: 
169: :dp:`fls_owdlsaaygtho`
170: A :t:`function signature` is a unique identification of a :t:`function`
171: that encompasses of its :t:`[function qualifier]s`, :t:`name`,
172: :t:`[generic parameter]s`, :t:`[function parameter]s`, :t:`return type`, and
173: :t:`where clause`.
174: 
175: :dp:`fls_2049qu3ji5x7`
176: A :t:`constant function` is a :t:`function` subject to :t:`keyword` ``const``.
177: 
178: :dp:`fls_7mlanuh5mvpn`
179: The :t:`function body` of a :t:`constant function` shall be a
180: :t:`constant expression`.

49: :dp:`fls_sye3d17l9bf5`
50: A :dt:`generic parameter` is a placeholder for a :t:`constant`, a :t:`lifetime`,
51: or a :t:`type`, whose :t:`constant`, :t:`lifetime`, or :t:`type` is supplied
52: statically by a :t:`generic argument`.
53: 
54: :dp:`fls_dalqke3rznrb`
55: All :s:`[LifetimeParameter]s` in a :s:`GenericParameterList` shall precede all
56: :s:`[ConstantParameter]s` and :s:`[TypeParameter]s`.
57: 
58: :dp:`fls_pi6eukz7kc99`
59: A :dt:`generic enum` is an :t:`enum` with :t:`[generic parameter]s`.
60: 
61: :dp:`fls_ixmgqupxvf73`
62: A :dt:`generic function` is a :t:`function` with :t:`[generic parameter]s`.
63: 
64: :dp:`fls_z311nxou9yi3`
65: A :dt:`generic implementation` is an :dt:`implementation` with

67: 
68: :dp:`fls_0GhOR32Z4j7M`
69: A :t:`generic type` is a :t:`type` with a :t:`generic parameter`.
70: 
71: :dp:`fls_wmcp0n36jlbr`
72: A :dt:`generic struct` is a :t:`struct` with :t:`[generic parameter]s`.
73: 
74: :dp:`fls_h42kg56vsefx`
75: A :dt:`generic trait` is a :t:`trait` with :t:`[generic parameter]s`.
76: 
77: :dp:`fls_372h3oevejih`
78: A :dt:`generic type alias` is a :t:`type alias` with :t:`[generic parameter]s`.
79: 
80: :dp:`fls_u8mqct93yimd`
81: A :dt:`generic union` is a :t:`union` with :t:`[generic parameter]s`.
82: 
83: :dp:`fls_vpcqgec83ybt`
84: A :t:`constant parameter` is a :t:`generic parameter` for a :t:`constant`.
85: 
86: :dp:`fls_3SjMBlc0b7qo`
87: A :t:`constant parameter initializer` is a :t:`construct` that provides the
88: default :t:`value` of its related :t:`constant parameter`.
89: 
90: :dp:`fls_p4yb8EAXlRU0`
91: A :t:`constant parameter initializer` shall be a :t:`constant expression`.
92: 
93: :dp:`fls_4a2qshaf5se7`
94: It is a static error to use a :t:`generic parameter` in the
95: :t:`discriminant initializer` of an :t:`enum variant`.
96: 
97: :dp:`fls_s0nrjwqg2wox`
98: A :t:`lifetime parameter` is a :t:`generic parameter` for a :t:`lifetime`.
99: 
100: :dp:`fls_2grtygcj8o3`
101: A :t:`lifetime parameter` shall not be used within a :t:`constant context`,
102: except for the ``'static`` :t:`lifetime`.

104: :dp:`fls_95eooah0vcqx`
105: A :t:`type parameter` is a :t:`generic parameter` for a :t:`type`.

107: :dp:`fls_ahCqtkh0m5sR`
108: A :dt:`type parameter initializer` is a :t:`construct` that provides the

112: The :t:`type` of the :t:`type parameter initializer` of a :t:`type parameter`
113: shall satisfy the :t:`[trait bound]s` of the :t:`type parameter`.
114: 
115: :dp:`fls_x4s7p2v981r6`
116: A :t:`generic enum` shall use all of its :t:`[type parameter]s` and
117: :t:`[lifetime parameter]s` at least once in at least one of its

123: 
124: :dp:`fls_6j616ydf2mnh`

126: :t:`[lifetime parameter]s` at least once in at least one of its :t:`[field]s`.
127: 
128: :dp:`fls_hyi2jnp38v1n`
129: A :t:`generic parameter` is said to :dt:`constrain` an :t:`implementation` if the

3151: * :dp:`fls_nw705fpon79b`
3152:   The :t:`type` of the :t:`index expression` is ``&mut T``, where ``T`` is
3153:   the element type of the :t:`indexed operand`'s :t:`type`.
3154: 
3155: :dp:`fls_fouu0z3jwoad`
3156: The :t:`value` of an :t:`index expression` is the indexed memory location.
3157: 
3158: .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
3159: 
3160: :dp:`fls_6sgj0ltt21i`
3161: The :t:`evaluation` of an :t:`index expression` proceeds as follows:
3162: 
3163: #. :dp:`fls_e5l4y3dy69xi`
3164:    The :t:`indexed operand` is evaluated.
3165: 
3166: #. :dp:`fls_fza3omn8yw7s`
3167:    The :t:`indexing operand` is evaluated.
3168: 
3169: #. :dp:`fls_ehamppbq4gmg`
3170:    If the :t:`index expression` is evaluated in a :t:`mutable place
3171:    expression context`, then :t:`expression`
3172:    ``*core::ops::IndexMut::index_mut(&mut indexed_operand, indexing_operand)``
3173:    is evaluated.
3174: 
3175: #. :dp:`fls_i68oxj659hc1`
3176:    Otherwise :t:`expression` ``*core::ops::Index::index(&indexed_operand,
3177:    indexing_operand)`` is evaluated.
3178: 
3179: .. rubric:: Examples
3180: 
3181: .. code-block:: rust
3182: 
3183:    let a = [[1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6], [7, 8, 9]];
3184:    a[1][2]
3185: 
3186: :dp:`fls_esvpmh6razg3`
3187: Evaluates to 6.
3188: 
3189: .. _fls_k64tfywtn0g8:
3190: 
3191: Tuple Expressions
3192: -----------------
3193: 
3194: .. rubric:: Syntax
3195: 
3196: .. syntax::
3197: 
3198:    TupleExpression ::=
3199:        $$($$ TupleInitializerList? $$)$$
3200: 
3201:    TupleInitializerList ::=
3202:        ExpressionList
3203: 
3204: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
3205: 
3206: :dp:`fls_87rp1hfwvjel`
3207: A :dt:`tuple expression` is an :t:`expression` that constructs a :t:`tuple`.
3208: 
3209: :dp:`fls_581y6jq1eyn8`
3210: A :dt:`tuple initializer` is an :t:`operand` that provides the :t:`value` of a
3211: :t:`tuple field` in a :t:`tuple expression`.
3212: 
3213: :dp:`fls_ljz3sxmfzflm`
3214: The :t:`type` of a :t:`tuple expression` is ``(T1, T2, ..., TN)``, where ``T1``
3215: is the :t:`type` of the first :t:`tuple initializer`, ``T2`` is the :t:`type` of
3216: the second :t:`tuple initializer`, and ``TN`` is the :t:`type` of the ``N``-th
3217: :t:`tuple initializer`.
3218: 
3219: :dp:`fls_k2aznqo9j49p`
3220: The :t:`value` of a :t:`tuple expression` is ``(V1, V2, ..., VN)``, where ``V1``
3221: is the :t:`value` of the first :t:`tuple initializer`, ``V2`` is the :t:`value`
3222: of the second :t:`tuple initializer`, and ``VN`` is the :t:`value` of the
3223: ``N``-th :t:`tuple initializer`.
3224: 
3225: .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
3226: 
3227: :dp:`fls_waf55yd3mpsq`
3228: The :t:`evaluation` of a :t:`tuple expression` evaluates its
3229: :t:`[tuple initializer]s` in left-to-right order.
3230: 
3231: .. rubric:: Examples
3232: 
3233: .. code-block:: rust
3234: 
3235:    ()
3236:    (1.2, 3.4)
3237:    ("hello", 42i16, true)
3238: 
3239: .. _fls_8tsynkj2cufj:

165: External Blocks
166: ---------------
167: 
168: .. rubric:: Syntax

170: .. syntax::
171:

177: 
178:    ExternItem ::=
179:        OuterAttributeOrDoc* (ExternalItemWithVisibility | TerminatedMacroInvocation)
180: 
181:    ExternalItemWithVisibility ::=
182:        VisibilityModifier? (
183:            FunctionDeclaration
184:          | StaticDeclaration
185:        )
186: 
187: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
188: 
189: :dp:`fls_4dje9t5y2dia`
190: An :dt:`external block` is a :t:`construct` that provides the declarations of
191: :t:`[external function]s` and :t:`[external static]s` as unchecked imports.
192: 
193: :dp:`fls_Nz0l16hMxqTd`
194: The :t:`ABI` of an :t:`external block` is determined as follows:
195: 
196: * :dp:`fls_4XOoiFloXM7t`
197:   If the :t:`external block` specifies an :s:`AbiKind`, then the :t:`ABI` is the specified :s:`AbiKind`.
198: 
199: * :dp:`fls_PBsepNHImJKH`
200:   Otherwise the :t:`ABI` is the :t:`extern C ABI`.
201:

203:

205: 
206:    extern "C" {
207:        static MAX_LENGTH: size_t;
208:

212:             compressed: *mut u8,

214:        fn log(msg: *const c_char, ...);

1124: Tuple Patterns
1125: ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
1126: 
1127: .. rubric:: Syntax
1128: 
1129: .. syntax::
1130: 
1131:    TuplePattern ::=
1132:        $$($$ PatternList? $$)$$
1133: 
1134: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
1135: 
1136: :dp:`fls_e2manugp4e0b`
1137: A :dt:`tuple pattern` is a :t:`pattern` that matches a :t:`tuple` which satisfies
1138: all criteria defined by its :t:`[subpattern]s`.
1139: 
1140: :dp:`fls_xk8udu4k61kj`
1141: A :t:`tuple pattern` is an :t:`irrefutable pattern` when all of its
1142: :t:`[subpattern]s` are :t:`[irrefutable pattern]s`.
1143: 
1144: :dp:`fls_yhcaz6v49ub2`
1145: The :t:`type` of a :t:`tuple pattern` is the :t:`type` of the :t:`tuple` being
1146: destructured.
1147: 
1148: :dp:`fls_v7c3y1q9m2bx`
1149: A :dt:`tuple field` is a :t:`field` of a :t:`tuple type`.
1150: 
1151: :dp:`fls_6WCm0Ra8NQl4`
1152: A :t:`subpattern` of a :t:`tuple pattern` matches a :t:`tuple field` of the
1153: :t:`tuple type` when its position and the position of the :t:`tuple field` in
1154: the :t:`tuple type` are the same. Such a :t:`subpattern` is a
1155: :dt:`matched tuple subpattern`.
1156: 
1157: :dp:`fls_a3qvQjyilORx`
1158: The position of a :t:`subpattern` is determined as follows:
1159: 
1160: * :dp:`fls_KmIHFxlBYelZ`
1161:   If the :t:`tuple pattern` has a :s:`RestPattern`, then
1162: 
1163:   * :dp:`fls_5bXqIaKiFcLg`
1164:     If the :t:`subpattern` precedes the :s:`RestPattern`, then its
1165:     position is the position within the :s:`PatternList` in left-to-right order,
1166:     starting from zero.
1167: 
1168:   * :dp:`fls_soHCAVfGlv5f`
1169:     If the :t:`subpattern` succeeds the :s:`RestPattern`, then its
1170:     position is the position within the :s:`PatternList` list in right-to-left
1171:     order, starting from the :t:`arity` of the :t:`tuple type` minus one.
1172: 
1173: * :dp:`fls_iiKvYs61959S`
1174:   Otherwise the position is the position within the :s:`PatternList` in
1175:   left-to-right order, starting from zero.
1176: 
1177: :dp:`fls_F4k6ljuP8Amf`
1178: The :t:`type` of the :t:`subpattern` of a :t:`tuple pattern` and the
1179: :t:`type` of the matched :t:`tuple field` shall be :t:`unifiable`.
1180: 
1181: :dp:`fls_GjjCDkVJPQS8`
1182: For each :t:`tuple field` of the :t:`tuple type`, the :t:`tuple pattern` shall
1183: either:
1184: 
1185: * :dp:`fls_9Qw9N87swwNe`
1186:   Contain at most one :t:`matched tuple subpattern`, or
1187: 
1188: * :dp:`fls_CQ84wkLyrAJv`
1189:   Have exactly one :s:`RestPattern`.
1190: 
1191: :dp:`fls_cC6ohNuiltfL`
1192: A :s:`RestPattern` is allowed even if all :t:`[tuple field]s` of the
1193: :t:`tuple type` have been matched.
1194: 
1195: .. rubric:: Examples
1196: 
1197: :dp:`fls_8r81vtv5hnrd`
1198: A tuple pattern in the context of a let statement.
1199:

1202:    let pair = (1, "two");
1203:    let (first, second) = pair;

97: A :t:`reference` is a :t:`value` of a :t:`reference type`. A :t:`reference`
98: can be obtained explicitly by using a :t:`borrow expression` or implicitly in
99: certain scenarios.
100: 
101: :dp:`fls_2s9h4k8v1qpm`
102: A :dt:`reference` is a :t:`value` of a :t:`reference type`.
103: 
104: :dp:`fls_tsqvr3fmcel`
105: A :dt:`referent` is the :t:`value` pointed-to by a :t:`reference`.
106: 
107: :dp:`fls_ev4a82fdhwr8`
108: A :t:`reference` shall point to an :t:`initialized` :t:`referent`.
109: 
110: :dp:`fls_cckf6dtkgwb4`
111: The :t:`lifetime` of a :t:`referent` shall be at least as long as the
112: :t:`lifetime` of its :t:`reference`.
113: 
114: :dp:`fls_8kqb8754e6p4`
115: A :t:`reference` is :dt:`active` from the point of obtaining its :t:`referent`
116: upto the last use of the :t:`reference`, prior to another assignment to the
117: :t:`reference` or the end of the :t:`scope` of the :t:`reference`.
118: 
119: :dp:`fls_v69rptdjao42`
120: A :t:`referent` shall not be :t:`passed <passing convention>` :t:`by move` while
121: a :t:`reference` to it is :t:`active`.
122: 
123: :dp:`fls_vg9h6tz6z37w`
124: A :t:`referent` shall not be modified while a :t:`reference` to it is
125: :t:`active`.
126: 
127: :dp:`fls_wcf5mxrzbujn`
128: An :t:`immutable reference` is a :t:`value` of a :t:`shared reference type`, and
129: prevents the mutation of its :t:`referent`.
130: 
131: :dp:`fls_4m2v8k1q9p0s`
132: A :t:`shared reference` is a :t:`value` of a :t:`shared reference type`.
133: 
134: :dp:`fls_fckoj1jh5mrc`
135: A :t:`mutable reference` is a :t:`value` of a :t:`mutable reference type`, and
136: allows the mutation of its :t:`referent`.
137: 
138: :dp:`fls_hqxsuyn285he`
139: The :t:`referent` of an :t:`immutable reference` shall be mutated only when the
140: :t:`type` of the :t:`referent` is subject to :t:`interior mutability`.
141: 
142: :dp:`fls_i1ny0k726a4a`
143: While a :t:`mutable reference` is :t:`active`, no other :t:`reference` shall
144: refer to a :t:`value` that :t:`[overlap]s` with the :t:`referent` of the
145: :t:`mutable reference`.
146: 
147: .. rubric:: Undefined Behavior
148: 
149: :dp:`fls_eT1hnLOx6vxk`
150: It is undefined behavior to access a :t:`value` through aliasing
151: :t:`[mutable reference]s` from :t:`unsafe context`.
152: 
153: .. rubric:: Examples
154: 
155: .. code-block:: rust
156: 
157:    let immutable_reference: &i32 = &42;
158:    let mutable_reference: &mut i32 = &mut 42;
159: 
160: .. _fls_a14slch83hzn:
161: 
162: Borrowing
163: ---------
164: 
165: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
166: 
167: :dp:`fls_c02flohk54pc`
168: :dt:`Borrowing` is the process of temporarily associating a :t:`reference` with a
169: :t:`value` without transferring :t:`ownership` permanently.
170: 
171: :dp:`fls_j9kof0px3l7s`
172: A :t:`borrow` is a :t:`reference` produced by :t:`borrowing`.
173: 
174: :dp:`fls_yL6cMahJUIqO`
175: A :t:`value` is :t:`borrowed` when it is associated with an active
176: :t:`borrow`.
177: 
178: :dp:`fls_wb031borrowed`
179: :dt:`borrowed` is the state of a memory location when a :t:`reference`
180: pointing to it is :t:`active`.
181: 
182: :dp:`fls_zepwytjwy049`
183: An :t:`implicit borrow` is a :t:`borrow` that is not present syntactically in
184: program text. An :t:`implicit borrow` occurs in the following contexts:
185: 
186: * :dp:`fls_nordokzfy36d`
187:   The :t:`call operand` of a :t:`call expression`,
188: 
189: * :dp:`fls_bjf3futso849`
190:   The :t:`[operand]s` of a :t:`comparison expression`,
191: 
192: * :dp:`fls_yfmy4v5zlgw9`
193:   The :t:`assigned operand` of a :t:`compound assignment expression`,
194: 
195: * :dp:`fls_jv18y618j2s3`
196:   The :t:`operand` of a :t:`field access expression`,
197: 
198: * :dp:`fls_g4i0jb27iryr`
199:   The :t:`operand` of a :t:`dereference expression`,
200: 
201: * :dp:`fls_kky9ufexrvaw`
202:   The :t:`indexed operand` of an :t:`index expression`,
203: 
204: * :dp:`fls_o5oq4jfswr4q`
205:   The :t:`receiver operand` of a :t:`method call expression`.
206: 
207: :dp:`fls_MT69AGRXH9pS``
208: An :t:`implicit borrow` may be an :dt:`immutable borrow` or a :t:`mutable borrow`
209: if required.

5057: Await Expressions
5058: -----------------
5059: 
5060: .. rubric:: Syntax
5061: 
5062: .. syntax::
5063: 
5064:    AwaitExpression ::=
5065:        FutureOperand $$.$$ $$await$$
5066: 
5067:    FutureOperand ::=
5068:        Operand
5069: 
5070: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
5071: 
5072: :dp:`fls_sjz5s71hwm7l`
5073: An :dt:`await expression` is an :t:`expression` that polls a :t:`future`,
5074: suspending the :t:`execution` of the :dt:`future` until the :t:`future` is ready.
5075: 
5076: :dp:`fls_vhchgab59jvd`
5077: A :dt:`future operand` is an :t:`operand` whose :t:`future` is being awaited by
5078: an :t:`await expression`.
5079: 
5080: :dp:`fls_k9pncajmhgk1`
5081: An :t:`await expression` shall appear within an
5082: :t:`async control flow boundary`. Only the innermost :t:`control flow boundary`
5083: shall be considered.
5084: 
5085: :dp:`fls_9uw5pd7kbrx3`
5086: The :t:`type` of a :t:`future operand` shall implement the
5087: :std:`core::future::IntoFuture` :t:`trait`.
5088: 
5089: :dp:`fls_c6mxfzef2qop`
5090: The :t:`type` of an :t:`await expression` is
5091: ``<_ as core::future::IntoFuture>::Output``.
5092: 
5093: :dp:`fls_l396mo6k9ox7`
5094: The :t:`value` of an :t:`await expression` is the :t:`value` held by
5095: :std:`core::task::Poll::Ready`.
5096: 
5097: .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
5098: 
5099: :dp:`fls_1ppywe40s62c`
5100: The :t:`evaluation` of an :t:`await expression` proceeds as follows:
5101: 
5102: #. :dp:`fls_eymcs2rgv3qw`
5103:    The :t:`future operand` is evaluated to a :t:`temporary` by invoking
5104:    :std:`core::future::IntoFuture::into_future` with the :t:`future operand`.
5105: 
5106: #. :dp:`fls_qshnnpirkasz`
5107:    The :t:`temporary` is pinned using :std:`core::pin::Pin::new_unchecked`.
5108: 
5109: #. :dp:`fls_umevprl99y6c`
5110:    The pinned :t:`temporary` is polled using :std:`core::future::Future::poll`,
5111:    passing in the :std:`core::task::Context` of the current task.
5112: 
5113: #. :dp:`fls_k76d8ady623m`
5114:    If :std:`core::future::Future::poll` returns
5115:    :std:`core::task::Poll::Pending`, then the current :t:`future` yields.
5116: 
5117: #. :dp:`fls_frwtufwe8dya`
5118:    If :std:`core::future::Future::poll` returns :std:`core::task::Poll::Ready`,
5119:    then
5120: 
5121:    #. :dp:`fls_u72ylhlmqge3`
5122:       The :t:`value` held within is unwrapped.
5123: 
5124:    #. :dp:`fls_tn3vwidct3ks`
5125:       Control stops the evaluation of the :t:`await expression`.
5126: 
5127: .. rubric:: Examples
5128:

5130: 
5131:    let future = async { expensive_function() };
5132:    future.await;

3851: Closure Expressions
3852: -------------------
3853: 
3854: .. rubric:: Syntax
3855: 
3856: .. syntax::
3857: 
3858:    ClosureExpression ::=
3859:        $$async$$? $$move$$? $$|$$ ClosureParameterList? $$|$$
3860:          (ClosureBody | ClosureBodyWithReturnType)
3861: 
3862:    ClosureBody ::=
3863:        Expression
3864: 
3865:    ClosureBodyWithReturnType ::=
3866:        ReturnTypeWithoutBounds BlockExpression
3867: 
3868:    ReturnTypeWithoutBounds ::=
3869:        $$->$$ TypeSpecificationWithoutBounds
3870: 
3871:    ClosureParameterList ::=
3872:        ClosureParameter ($$,$$ ClosureParameter)* $$,$$?
3873: 
3874:    ClosureParameter ::=
3875:        OuterAttributeOrDoc* PatternWithoutAlternation TypeAscription?
3876: 
3877: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
3878: 
3879: :dp:`fls_2d141c9a0yui`
3880: A :dt:`closure expression` is an :t:`expression` that defines a
3881: :t:`closure type` and constructs a value of that :t:`type`.
3882: 
3883: :dp:`fls_My6pMgpeFCFg`
3884: An :dt:`async closure expression` is a :t:`closure expression` subject to keyword ``async`` that defines an :t:`async closure type` and constructs a value of that :t:`type`.
3885: 
3886: :dp:`fls_7ln7IejTjdmi`
3887: An :dt:`async closure type` is the :t:`closure type` defined by an
3888: :t:`async closure expression`.
3889: 
3890: :dp:`fls_UgJgur0z6d4a`
3891: The :dt:`return type` of a :t:`closure type` is determined as follows:
3892: 
3893: * :dp:`fls_af1WL2mBKMfW`
3894:   If the :t:`closure expression` specifies a :s:`ClosureBodyWithReturnType`, then the :t:`return type` is the specified :s:`ReturnTypeWithoutBounds`.
3895: 
3896: * :dp:`fls_wLVeE6cNG8oa`
3897:   Otherwise the :t:`return type` is the :t:`type` of the :t:`closure body`.
3898: 
3899: :dp:`fls_3aKZB0ILIkZw`
3900: An :dt:`anonymous return type` is an :t:`impl trait type` ascribed to a
3901: :t:`function` :t:`return type`.
3902: 
3903: :dp:`fls_DSy7bPKGzyov`
3904: The :t:`return type` of an :t:`async closure type` is an :t:`anonymous return type` with a :std:`core::future::Future` :t:`trait bound` and a :t:`binding argument` for the ``Output`` :t:`associated type alias` with the actual :t:`return type` of the corresponding :t:`closure type`.
3905: 
3906: :dp:`fls_srbl7ptknjyk`
3907: A :dt:`closure body` is a :t:`construct` that represents the executable portion
3908: of a :t:`closure expression`.
3909: 
3910: :dp:`fls_oey0ivaiu1l`
3911: A :t:`closure body` denotes a new :t:`control flow boundary`.
3912: 
3913: :dp:`fls_fg8lx0yyt6oq`
3914: A :t:`closure body` is subject to :t:`capturing`.
3915: 
3916: :dp:`fls_c3rzwUxjmBMY`
3917: A :dt:`closure parameter` is a :t:`construct` that yields a set of
3918: :t:`[binding]s` that bind matched input :t:`[value]s` to :t:`[name]s` at the

metadata.preview:
.. rubric:: Legality Rules

:dp:`fls_tlwr4u7bjhh5`
:t:`Pattern matching` that involves a :t:`pattern` and a context :t:`value`
proceeds as follows:

#. :dp:`fls_tZJgZDWVChJV`
   If the :t:`pattern` is an :t:`or-pattern`, then perform :t:`pattern matching` with each :t:`pattern-without-alternation` of the :t:`or-pattern` and the :t:`value`. If any such :t:`pattern matching` succeeds, then the overall :t:`pattern matching` succeeds.

#. :dp:`fls_62626ws222op`
   If the :t:`pattern` is an :t:`identifier pattern`, then perform :t:`identifier pattern matching`.

#. :dp:`fls_q0z46h1gnzez`
   If the :t:`pattern` is a :t:`literal pattern`, then perform :t:`literal pattern matching`.


#. :dp:`fls_1r0vm6rg13o9`
   If the :t:`pattern` is a :t:`parenthesized pattern`, then perform :t:`parenthesized pattern matching`.

152: Statics
153: -------
154: 
155: .. rubric:: Syntax
156: 
157: .. syntax::
158: 
159:    StaticDeclaration ::=
160:        ItemSafety? $$static$$ $$mut$$? Name TypeAscription StaticInitializer? $$;$$
161: 
162:    StaticInitializer ::=
163:        $$=$$ Expression
164: 
165: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
166: 
167: :dp:`fls_ibrmiwfypldh`
168: A :dt:`static` is a :t:`value` that is associated with a specific memory
169: location.
170: 
171: :dp:`fls_mt94jvoot9dx`
172: A :t:`static` defined within a :t:`generic function` exists once in the
173: output executable or library.
174: 
175: :dp:`fls_k0r2c6uq29tu`
176: The :t:`type specification` of a :t:`static` shall have ``'static``
177: :t:`lifetime`.
178: 
179: :dp:`fls_b6ods85htuyn`
180: The :t:`type` of a :t:`static` shall implement the :std:`core::marker::Sized`
181: :t:`trait`.
182: 
183: :dp:`fls_WRpcVF1fLEpr`
184: A :t:`static` shall only be subject to an :s:`ItemSafety` if it is an :t:`external static` in an :t:`unsafe external block`.
185: 
186: :dp:`fls_doi4z6u55bi7`
187: A :t:`mutable static` is a :t:`static` with :t:`keyword` ``mut`` whose
188: :t:`value` can be modified.
189: 
190: :dp:`fls_74hp208pto22`
191: Access to a :t:`mutable static` shall require :t:`unsafe context`.
192: 
193: :dp:`fls_jfde2vg6mtww`
194: An :dt:`immutable static` is a :t:`static` whose :t:`value` cannot be modified.
195: 
196: :dp:`fls_k4tyqb1j6zjo`
197: The type of an :t:`immutable static` shall implement the
198: :std:`core::marker::Sync` :t:`trait`.
199: 
200: :dp:`fls_t17h5h6a6v4c`
201: A :t:`static initializer` is a :t:`construct` that provides the :t:`value` of
202: its related :t:`static`.
203: 
204: :dp:`fls_yq0hpy4jx2qb`
205: A :t:`static` shall have a :t:`static initializer`, unless it is an
206: :t:`external static`.
207: 
208: :dp:`fls_vgidvfwzm4ks`
209: The :t:`expression` of a :t:`static initializer` shall be a
210: :t:`constant expression`.
211: 
212: :dp:`fls_8dcldbvu7lav`
213: A use of a :t:`static` is a :t:`place expression` referring to the unique
214: location of the :t:`static`.
215: 
216: .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
217: 
218: :dp:`fls_w0nb0mphho7b`
219: The :t:`elaboration` of a :t:`static` evaluates its :t:`static initializer`.
220: 
221: :dp:`fls_eeocxst9vafn`
222: All :t:`[path]s` that refer to a :t:`static` refer to the same memory location.
223: 
224: :dp:`fls_47khd5ljsxeq`
225: A :t:`static` is not :t:`dropped` during :t:`destruction`.
226: 
227: :dp:`fls_dowxbphqvk3n`
228: An :t:`immutable static` whose :t:`type` is not subject to
229: :t:`interior mutability` may reside in read-only memory.

:dp:`fls_9kk81isk0mlp`
The :t:`range pattern low bound` of an :t:`inclusive range pattern` shall be
less than or equal to its :t:`range pattern high bound`.

:dp:`fls_8bdOqkO1NuJW`
The :t:`range pattern low bound` of an :t:`exclusive range pattern` shall be
less than its :t:`range pattern high bound`.
